text: ( iii ) The expression ρ silent ( θ ) = 2 θ − 1 4 θ − 2 θ 2 − 1 for the exponential degree distribution can be obtained by plugging p 1 ( θ ) = 1 − θ and μ 1 ( θ ) = 1 − θ into (30). Thus, ( iii ) follows immediately from Proposition 5 ( i ) . ( iv ) The value b ρ silence ≈ 0 . 0685 can be determined by nding numerically the solution to the equation min θ ∈ [0 , 1] φ ( ρ, θ ) = 1 , both sides of which are plotted in Figure 6. From Figure 6, one can see that the stability condition (33) holds for a non-empty set of the network density levels i ρ < b ρ silence . That set is given by { θ : φ ( ρ, θ ) < 1 } and is an open interval � θ silent , θ silent  because φ ( ρ, θ ) has a unique minimizer w.r.t θ , hence it is quasi-convex with respect to θ . The rest of the proof follows from Propositions 5 and 6. This proves ( iv ) and completes the proof. □
